raw_text:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "Methadone is a highly effective medication that eases the debilitating symptoms of opioid withdrawal for people who have quit drugs like heroin and fentanyl.",
    "California has the most restrictive methadone laws in the country.",
    "The state's stringent regulations on methadone treatment far surpass federal guidelines.",
    "Many European countries have made easy access to methadone a cornerstone of their addiction fighting strategy and have been highly successful in reversing their opioid crises.",
    "In 2023, California alone had over 7,300 deaths from opioid overdose.",
    "Removing unnecessary barriers to care can help more Californians receive life-saving treatment for opioid addiction.",
    "AB 2115 allows doctors to prescribe their patients up to 72-hours worth of take-home doses of methadone for the first time.",
    "AB 2115 increases the amount of methadone a patient can take home from a clinic.",
    "AB 2115 allows for expedited entry into treatment programs by permitting non-methadone clinic doctors to perform federally required physical exams.",
    "AB 2115 removes the requirement for patients to have at least one year of recorded opioid usage before receiving treatment.",
    "AB 2115 removes the requirement for frequent counseling services for patients.",
    "AB 2115 allows patients to be absent from treatment for up to 30 days before being removed from a program.",
    "AB 2115 gives physicians greater discretion to determine the appropriate dosage of methadone for patients."
  ]
}
```
